title: DAG-ML TrainingOutcome v1
description: Portable result of compile/plan/FIT_CV/SELECT and optional REFIT. Semantic validation closes transitively from bound outputs through node_plans.input_nodes and requires exact patch, influence, lineage, artifact and cache coverage. It contains no runtime handle and is the exact object bound by PredictorBinding.training_outcome_fingerprint.

training_request_fingerprint.description: Exact fingerprint of the signed TrainingRequest executed to produce this outcome.

data_identities.description: Strictly sorted exact data identities attested by RuntimeDataProvider before controller execution.

effective_plan_fingerprint.description: DAG-ML TCV1 SHA-256 of effective_plan. Existing graph/campaign/controller/params fingerprints keep their historical serde_json algorithms.

selection_output_id.description: OutputBinding id whose producer-specific averaged FIT_CV reports were ranked to produce the selection decision.

parameter_patches.description: Canonical order is (node_id, namespace, path); duplicate targets are invalid. The array is exactly the selected variant's recursively materialized param_overrides leaves.

score_set.description: Mandatory OOF evaluation reports used by SELECT; TrainingOutcome v1 always represents compile through FIT_CV and SELECT.

lineage.description: Sorted portable lineage. Semantic validation requires one FIT_CV record per supported predictor-closure node/fold and, after completed refit, one REFIT record per supported closure node with exact upstream and artifact references.

portable_prediction_caches.description: When execution_bundle carries prediction caches, this payload set is non-null and its requirement keys and block fingerprints match exactly. A no-refit outcome may retain OOF caches for later REFIT replay.

replayable_phases.description: Phases this outcome can honestly replay, deterministically derived from the full effective predictor closure and the retained artifacts/caches, never from the refit flag alone. Canonical order is REFIT, PREDICT, EXPLAIN and validation enforces exact order and set equality against a re-derivation. A closure node requires retained inference state exactly when its controller capabilities are Stateful OR EmitsArtifacts; the artifact_policy value (including ReplayRequired) and fit_scope alone never imply retained state, so a stateless deterministic operator (e.g. a replay_required transform that recomputes at inference) needs no retained artifact. A completed refit never re-advertises REFIT; it exposes PREDICT and/or EXPLAIN only when every closure node supports that phase and every state-retaining (Stateful or EmitsArtifacts) closure node has a retained refit artifact. A skipped refit exposes REFIT only when every closure node supports REFIT and every closure requires_oof dependency is backed by a self-contained retained cache record and portable payload. The empty array is valid and is preferable to advertising a false capability.

outcome_fingerprint.description: DAG-ML TCV1 SHA-256 of this complete portable outcome with only outcome_fingerprint omitted.
